title: Playtest Closure, Release Gates, Rollback, Recovery, and Production Defense · Behavior Case: Playtest Closure
summary: A polished demo becomes release by copying the latest executable; no one owns accessibility findings, performance budgets, package provenance, crash recovery, rollback, or support. Predict player-visible behavior, state, units, timing, input, rendering, feedback, failure, accessibility, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the playtest closure increment in production game release and defense dossier. Learners progress through prediction, explicit th…
objectives: Trace each observed barrier or defect through cause, priority, repair, regression evidence, retest, disposition, and owner.
Minimize diagnostics, redact paths and identifiers, require informed opt-in for telemetry, and document retention and deletion.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates.

content.code:
# Worked focus: asteroids-playtest-closure — Trace each observed barrier or defect through cause, priority, repair, regression evidence, retest, disposition, and owner.
# Evidence: asteroids-worked-asteroids-release-recovery-defense-5
# Competency: asteroids-worked-asteroids-release-recovery-defense-5.
# Case 9036: a controller-remapping accessibility review.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save.
def worked_asteroids_release_recovery_defense_5(correctness: bool = True, accessibility: bool = True, performance: bool = True, portability: bool = True, security: bool = True, rollback: bool = True, recovery: bool = True, residual_risk_owned: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "playerworked5",
        "seed9036",
        "input5dekrow",
        "frameworked59036",
        "state9036worked5",
        "playtest5dekrow9036",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    gates = (correctness, accessibility, performance, portability, security, rollback, recovery)
    passed = sum(1 for gate in gates if gate)
    if not correctness or not accessibility:
        return False, passed, "player-quality"
    if not performance or not portability or not security:
        return False, passed, "release-quality"
    if not rollback or not recovery:
        return False, passed, "recovery-gap"
    if not residual_risk_owned:
        return False, passed, "unowned-risk"
    assert passed == len(gates)
    return True, passed, "production-game-defense"

# Evidence: asteroids-playtest-closure-hyq3n6
# Competency: asteroids-playtest-closure.
# Case 9562: a keyboard-only community arcade night.
# Operating constraint: test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases.
# Changed case: run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save.
def evidence_asteroids_playtest_closure_hyq3n6(correctness: bool = True, accessibility: bool = True, performance: bool = True, portability: bool = True, security: bool = True, rollback: bool = True, recovery: bool = True, residual_risk_owned: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:

    assert evidence_variant_hyq3n6
    evidence_axes_hyq3n6 = (
        "playerhyq3n6",
        "seed9562",
        "input6n3qyh",
        "framehyq3n69562",
        "state9562hyq3n6",
        "playtest6n3qyh9562",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_hyq3n6)) == 6
    gates = (correctness, accessibility, performance, portability, security, rollback, recovery)
    passed = sum(1 for gate in gates if gate)
    if not correctness or not accessibility:
        return False, passed, "player-quality"
    if not performance or not portability or not security:
        return False, passed, "release-quality"
    if not rollback or not recovery:
        return False, passed, "recovery-gap"
    if not residual_risk_owned:
        return False, passed, "unowned-risk"
    assert passed == len(gates)
    return True, passed, "production-game-defense"

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Playtest Closure structural check is only one gate. production game release and defense dossier must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

hints: For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Build Playtest Closure evidence · Theory Playtest Closure, name the player, revision, runtime, seed, input, time, coordinate, state, entity, collision, render, cue, accessibility, package, release, and desktop-transfer boundary, Playtest Closure precondition, or invariant that must hold.

Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Trace each observed barrier or defect through cause, priority, repair, regression evidence, retest, disposition, and owner.
